revision.text:
{{Short description|Ancient tomb in Hamada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sufu Kofun | native_name =周布古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sufu | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Hamada, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of [[Hamada, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and [[Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[Sufu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hamada, Shimane|Hamada, Shimane]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hamada, Shimane|Hamada, Shimane]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]

Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Hamada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sufu Kofun | native_name =周布古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sufu | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Hamada, Shimane|Hamada, Shimane]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hamada, Shimane|Hamada, Shimane]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hamada, Shimane|Hamada, Shimane]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Hamada, Shimane|Hamada, Shimane]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the [[:en:Iwami Province|Iwami region]].
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]]. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from [[:en:Sufu Station|Sufu Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from {{ill|Sufu Station|en|Sufu Station|WD=Q3701458|lt=Sufu Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Hamada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name =Sufu Kofun | native_name =周布古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sufu | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hamada, Shimane|en|Hamada, Shimane|WD=Q432055|lt=Hamada, Shimane}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Hamada, Shimane|en|Hamada, Shimane|WD=Q432055|lt=Hamada, Shimane}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami region}}.
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from {{ill|Sufu Station|en|Sufu Station|WD=Q3701458|lt=Sufu Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Sufu Kofun]] {{Short description|Ancient tomb in Hamada, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Sufu Kofun | native_name =周布古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sufu | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Hamada, Shimane|en|Hamada, Shimane|WD=Q432055|lt=Hamada, Shimane|1=Hamada, Shimane}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|51|18|N|132|01|05|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sufu Kofun'''|周布古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Jiwa neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Hamada, Shimane|en|Hamada, Shimane|WD=Q432055|lt=Hamada, Shimane|1=Hamada, Shimane}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1936.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/139144|title=周布古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 10, 2023}}</ref> It is the second largest [[kofun]] in the {{ill|Iwami Province|en|Iwami Province|WD=Q1053709|lt=Iwami region|1=Iwami Province}}.
==Overview== The Sufu Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in a hilly area at an elevation of about 40 meters overlooking the Sufu Plain and the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}. It used to be called 'Hisagozuka' or 'Onguro'. There was formerly another tumulus called "Menguro Kofun" about 170 meters north of the Sufu Kofun, but it was destroyed due to the development of {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s. The Sufu Kofun is about 65 meters long. Its posterior circular portion has a diameter of 42 meters and height of six meters and its anterior rectangular portion has a width of 25 meters and height of five meters. Portions of the antierior portion have been cut away due to the encroachment of residential development despite its protected status; but if intact the total length would have been about 69 meters. Many fist-sized river stones used as ''[[fukiishi]]'' remain on the slope, and fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} can also be seen. From these artifacts, is thought that the tumulus was constructed at the end of the middle Kofun period (around the latter half of the 5th century).
The tumulus is located about five minutes on foot from {{ill|Sufu Station|en|Sufu Station|WD=Q3701458|lt=Sufu Station|1=Sufu Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.hamada.shimane.jp/www/contents/1392969350230/index.html Hamada City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
